meaning: vt.为本党利益改划选举区分vi.不公正操纵,欺骗n.改变选举区,欺骗
examples: Even apolitically drawn boundaries can unintentionally gerrymander.
First, the state's demographics are changing fast enough that an overly ambitious partisan gerrymander is likely to come unstuck during its ten-year lifespan.
Both parties said the ban was an attempt by Labour to "gerrymander" assembly elections.
Leaving that out is an odd way to gerrymander the gang, he says.
